summary: This is a brief link-roundup post where Joel Spolsky promotes a magazine article he wrote about automatically tracking software crashes in the field, shares a quote of his about hiring philosophy that appeared on MSN, and links back to his longer piece on interviewing. The post is essentially a collection of self-promotional pointers rather than a standalone argument.
key_points: Joel wrote an article for STQE Magazine about automatically tracking software crashes that occur in production
His hiring philosophy centers on using interview questions as a pretext for having interesting conversations to evaluate candidates
He distinguishes between the surface-level interview question and the real goal of assessing how a person thinks through conversation
money_quotes: If you have an interesting conversation about certain types of topics with a person, you can determine if he is the type of person you want to hire.
The questions are almost a pretext to having that conversation.
key_insight: Interview questions serve primarily as a pretext for having a meaningful conversation that reveals whether a candidate thinks the way you need them to.
